title: Insilico + Human Longevity sign multi-million-dollar deal to co-develop industry-first AI longevity foundation model
description: Insilico Medicine (3696.HK) and Human Life Foundation Models, a new subsidiary of J. Craig Venter's Human Longevity, formally announced a multi-million-dollar collaboration to build large-scale AI foundation models for human longevity — combining Insilico's Pharma.AI generative platform with HLI's multi-omic and clinical dataset. This is the strongest signal yet that AI drug discovery and clinical longevity data are converging into a single commercial platform.

title: STAT longevity-as-industry survey: $8.5B 2024 capital, XPRIZE Healthspan + FDA Reagan-Udall talks signal regulatory turn
description: STAT's dispatch from Vitalist Bay confirms the longevity field has shifted from movement to industry, citing 2024 global investments doubling to $8.5B, XPRIZE Healthspan operating at scale, and FDA's Reagan-Udall Foundation now engaged with anti-aging regulators. Researcher Jamie Justice told the audience optimism is warranted because 'we have their attention.'

title: BGE-102 and UDP-003 cement two distinct druggable inflammaging axes
description: Within 72 hours, BioAge's BGE-102 (NLRP3 inhibitor, 83-86% hsCRP reduction) and Cyclarity's UDP-003 (7-ketocholesterol clearance) both reported clean Phase 1 safety with biomarker engagement — establishing NLRP3 and cholesterol-oxidation damage as the two most clinically advanced inflammaging targets entering 2H 2026.

title: Bezos vs Thiel longevity portfolios reframed as bull case for Altos, Unity, Sana — $314B therapeutic market projection
description: Social Life Magazine catalog of Bezos and Thiel longevity bets — Altos ($3B), Unity, Nautilus, Sana, Denali plus Methuselah Foundation — anchors a $314B-by-2030 therapeutic-segment forecast and a 28% share of 2024 capital flowing to discovery platforms. The piece crystallizes how concentrated billionaire backing has become a structural feature of the field.

title: Tozorakimab COPD Phase III win extends inflammaging franchise into respiratory aging
description: AstraZeneca's anti-IL-33 antibody tozorakimab met its primary endpoint in the Phase III MIRANDA COPD trial, the first major Phase III readout positioning an inflammaging mechanism for a primary geriatric pulmonary disease and reinforcing IL-33/ST2 as a durable druggable node across aging tissues.

title: Insilico Medicine and Human Longevity announce collaboration to co-develop industry-first AI foundation model for longevity science
description: Insilico Medicine and newly-launched Human Life Foundation Models (a Human Longevity subsidiary) signed a multi-million-dollar deal to build large-scale foundation models combining Insilico's Pharma.AI with HLI's multi-omic clinical data, aiming for commercial deployment in predictive health and aging drug discovery.

title: STAT: Longevity movement shifts from fringe idea to big business
description: Field dispatch from Vitalist Bay documents the transition of longevity from movement to industry, with $8.5B in 2024 funding, mainstream investor presence, and FDA's Reagan-Udall Foundation engaging on anti-aging drug regulation alongside XPRIZE Healthspan and ARPA-H.

title: AstraZeneca tozorakimab meets primary endpoint in Phase III MIRANDA COPD trial
description: Anti-IL-33 antibody tozorakimab hit primary endpoint in Phase III COPD trial, validating the IL-33/ST2 inflammaging axis in a major geriatric respiratory disease and accelerating AZ's late-stage immunology longevity-adjacent pipeline.

title: Bezos & Thiel longevity bets: how Silicon Valley structures the $314B aging market
description: Detailed catalog of Bezos (Altos Labs $3B, Unity, Nautilus, Sana, Denali) and Thiel (Methuselah Foundation $3.5M, Unity co-investor) longevity portfolios, with projection that longevity therapeutics will reach $314B by 2030 and discovery platforms alone took 28% of 2024 longevity capital.

title: Insilico forms longevity board chaired by Eli Lilly's Andrew Adams to oversee AI aging research
description: Insilico's newly-announced longevity advisory board, chaired by Eli Lilly Group VP of Molecular Discovery Andrew Adams, brings pharma-grade governance to the company's aging-focused drug discovery pipeline ahead of the Human Longevity collaboration.

title: BioAge BGE-102 Phase 1 cuts hsCRP 83-86%, Phase 2 in H1 2026
description: BioAge Labs reported clean Phase 1 safety and biomarker engagement for oral NLRP3 inhibitor BGE-102 with 87-93% of participants reaching hsCRP <2 mg/L; Phase 2 cardiovascular risk trial planned for H1 2026 with readout H2 2026.

title: Cyclarity UDP-003 Phase 1: first human evidence 7-ketocholesterol can be safely cleared
description: Randomized double-blind Phase 1 in 84 participants showed UDP-003 dose-dependently increased urinary 7KC excretion with a 3-hour half-life and no serious adverse events, providing first clinical proof that the 7KC atherosclerosis-damage molecule is druggable.

title: Retro Biosciences trial 'going super good', Phase 1 data expected August
description: Retro CEO Joe Betts-LaCroix told STAT's Breakthrough Summit West that the company's autophagy-focused Alzheimer's Phase 1 has shown no dose-limiting toxicities and initial data is expected around August, anchoring the protein-clearance modality after the $1.8B valuation raise.

title: Inbrain completes enrollment in first human graphene brain study
description: Inbrain Neuroelectronics completed patient enrollment in the first-in-human study of its graphene brain-computer interface, a platform with direct relevance to neurodegenerative disease and brain-aging interventions.

title: AlzeCure completes Phase Ib study of ACD856
description: AlzeCure Pharma completed Phase Ib of NeuroRestore candidate ACD856, advancing a TrkA/B-positive allosteric modulator approach for Alzheimer's and brain-aging targets toward Phase 2.

title: AB Science links masitinib to survival benefit in ALS trial
description: AB Science reported survival benefit signal for masitinib in ALS, extending the tyrosine kinase inhibitor's case as a multi-indication geroprotective agent in neurodegenerative disease.

title: Seragon SRN-901 extends median lifespan in mice — published study
description: Seragon Biosciences disclosed lifespan-extension data for SRN-901 in murine studies, expanding the post-ITP geroprotector candidate roster with a new published mouse-lifespan dataset.

title: Sheba Medical study: bio-age gap predicts mortality and hospitalization
description: Sheba Medical Center study finds each year by which biological age exceeds chronological age is associated with 15% higher mortality and 6% more hospitalizations, after adjusting for BMI, smoking, and hypertension — strengthening the clinical case for bio-age clocks as screening biomarkers.

title: Buffalo team: TTP restoration reverses frailty in old mice
description: Engineering elderly mice to maintain TTP (tristetraprolin) — an RNA-binding protein that degrades pro-inflammatory transcripts — restored grip strength, gait, bone health, and immune profile; Blackshear lab is now screening small molecules that boost TTP expression.

title: Common diabetes drug linked to exceptional longevity in women
description: Observational analysis links a commonly prescribed diabetes drug with substantially elevated odds of reaching exceptional old age in women, reinforcing the case for metformin-class agents as candidate geroprotectors.

title: Florida emerges as US hotbed for biological age reversal clinics
description: Sun Sentinel report finds Florida has become a major US center for clinics offering interventions to slow or reverse biological age, paralleling rapid expansion of consumer longevity infrastructure documented at Vitalist Bay.

title: Public health editorial: 'from prevention to system-wide resilience' in age of longevity interventions
description: New editorial in a public health journal argues longevity interventions require system-wide resilience frameworks rather than disease-by-disease prevention, signaling academic public health is engaging with the geroscience agenda.

title: Fight Aging: microglial senescence emerging as central Alzheimer's driver
description: Fight Aging review consolidates evidence that microglial senescence is a tractable driver of Alzheimer's pathology, supporting brain-resident senolytic strategies as a near-term therapeutic axis.

title: Fight Aging: WHO physical activity guidelines reaffirmed as baseline geroprotector
description: Fight Aging summarizes new epidemiological work reinforcing WHO physical activity guidelines (150 min/week moderate or 75 min vigorous + resistance) as the most evidence-backed baseline geroprotective protocol for older adults.

title: Fight Aging: atherosclerosis therapies pivot toward damage-clearance vs LDL alone
description: Fight Aging argues atherosclerosis intervention is shifting from pure LDL lowering toward direct clearance of aging-damage molecules like 7-ketocholesterol — citing Cyclarity's UDP-003 Phase 1 as the inflection point.

title: Vitalist Bay debates: NeuroAge dementia screening, organ-replacement timelines, NeoRhythm/Somnee biohacking
description: Vitalist Bay sessions surfaced NeuroAge brain MRI + blood biomarker dementia screening, KindBio xenotransplantation ethics, and consumer devices including NeoRhythm ($299) and Somnee ($489) headsets as protocol-layer interventions now reaching enthusiast adoption.

title: Wikipedia timeline of aging research consolidates 2025-26 milestones
description: Wikipedia's Timeline of Aging Research entry consolidated 2025-26 milestones including Yamanaka-factor reprogramming results, GlyNAC lifespan extension, MPTR reprogramming, and Procyanidin C1 healthspan data — useful longitudinal index for field readers.

title: Alex Zhavoronkov named to Business Insider's most innovative leaders list
description: Insilico CEO Alex Zhavoronkov recognized as one of the most innovative leaders in healthcare by Business Insider, with Insilico now responsible for an estimated 70-80% of AI-generated drugs entering clinical trials.

title: Cell Press: Hallmarks of Aging abstract submissions close June 5 for 2026 conference
description: Cell Press reminded researchers that abstract submissions for the 2026 Hallmarks of Aging conference close Friday June 5, marking the next field-wide consolidation moment for the framework now considered the field's dominant organizing paradigm.

title: Pharma services M&A themes: longevity emerging as discrete deal category
description: Skytale Group analysis flags longevity as one of three themes reshaping pharma services M&A in 2026, alongside AI-powered CRO services and specialty pharmacy, signaling investor classification of longevity as a standalone industry segment.
